title: Haxchi
requirements: A Wii U on the latest firmware (5.5.6 or lower in North America, 5.5.5 or lower elsewhere)
A legitimately purchased, digitally installed DS Virtual Console game from the eShop
An SD card formatted as FAT32 (a microSD with an adapter is fine)
The Wii U connected to the internet
warnings: Haxchi is a legacy method. Unless you specifically need it, Aroma is the recommended way to hack a Wii U today.
Every time you modify a Wii U there is a small chance of an unrecoverable brick. Follow each step exactly.
The DS VC game you inject Haxchi into stops being playable until you uninstall Haxchi and reinstall the game.
Make a NAND backup when the guide tells you to. Don't skip it.
_note: Legacy Haxchi method, transcribed from the old homebrew-guide (docsify) Wii U guide. Files are downloaded by hand from the linked GitHub releases — Graphite does not automate these legacy downloads.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
**Haxchi** injects custom firmware into a paid DS Virtual Console game. Launching that game from the Wii U Menu boots into CFW. It has to be re-launched every reboot to re-enable CFW (if you want it to boot automatically, use **CBHC** instead).

Haxchi requires a **legitimately installed, paid DS VC game** bought from the eShop. Modern hacking uses **Aroma** instead — only follow this if you have a specific reason.

First, make sure your Wii U is on the **latest firmware** and your SD card is **FAT32** (Graphite verifies this below). Never name the SD card `wiiu`.

steps.title: Choose and buy a DS VC game
steps.body:
Pick a compatible DS Virtual Console game and buy it from the eShop as a **digital download** (it must install to the console). The game you inject won't be playable until you later uninstall Haxchi.

If you don't already own one, **Brain Age** (North America) / **Brain Training** (Europe) is usually the cheapest and least missed.

Compatible games include: Animal Crossing: Wild World, Big Brain Academy, Brain Age / Brain Training, Donkey Kong: Jungle Climber, the Kirby DS titles, The Legend of Zelda: Phantom Hourglass / Spirit Tracks, Mario & Luigi: Partners in Time, Mario Kart DS, New Super Mario Bros., Pokémon Mystery Dungeon: Explorers of Sky, Star Fox Command, Super Mario 64 DS, Wario: Master of Disguise, WarioWare: Touched!, Yoshi's Island DS, and Yoshi Touch & Go.

(European consoles may already own Dr. Kawashima's Brain Training free — check your previously downloaded titles first.)

steps.title: Put the files on your SD card
steps.body:
Graphite downloads the latest homebrew and unpacks each piece into the right folder on your SD card:

- The **Homebrew Launcher** and its installer payload
- **WUP Installer GX2** and the **Homebrew Launcher Channel**
- The **Wii U NAND Dumper** and the **Homebrew App Store** installer
- **Haxchi**

This is everything the guide would normally have you download by hand. Just press the button and wait.

steps.title: Run the browser exploit
steps.body:
Put the SD card back into the **Wii U** and make sure it's online.

Open the **Internet Browser** and go to `wiiuexploit.xyz`. Tap **Run Homebrew Launcher!** — the console should boot into the **Homebrew Launcher**.

If the screen freezes or goes white, wait a few seconds; if nothing happens, reboot, clear the browser's saved data, and try again.

steps.title: Make a NAND backup
steps.body:
This is your safety net. In the Homebrew Launcher, open **Wii U NAND Dumper**.

Set it up like the picture: **SLC: Yes, SLCCMPT: Yes, OTP: Yes, SEEPROM: Yes**. Leave **MLC** on **No** unless your SD card is bigger than the console's storage — MLC is optional and not needed to fix most bricks.

Press **A** to dump. When it finishes, power off, move the card to your computer, and copy `slc.bin`, `slccmpt.bin`, `seeprom.bin`, `otp.bin` (and any `mlc.bin.part` files) somewhere safe. Then delete them from the card to free space.

steps.title: Install Haxchi and launch CFW
steps.body:
Put the card back in the Wii U and run the browser exploit again to reach the Homebrew Launcher.

1. Open the **Haxchi** app.
2. With the D-Pad, highlight the DS VC game you bought and press **A** to inject Haxchi into it.
3. When it finishes, you'll return to the Wii U Menu and the game's icon is now a **Haxchi** icon.
4. Launch the modified game — the console reboots with **CFW enabled**.

CFW is temporary: you must **run the Haxchi game every reboot** to re-enable it. You can now re-attach any USB devices.

steps.title: Install the Homebrew Launcher Channel (optional)
steps.body:
This adds a Homebrew Launcher icon to the Wii U Menu so you don't have to hold **A**. It's optional — you can always open the Launcher by holding **A** while launching the Haxchi game.

1. Launch the Haxchi game to enable CFW, then launch it again **holding A** to open the Homebrew Launcher.
2. Open **WUP Installer GX2**.
3. Select `Homebrew Launcher Channel`, press **Install**, confirm **Yes**.
4. Choose **NAND** as the destination.
5. When done, press **Home** until you're back at the Wii U Menu — the channel now appears there.

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
Haxchi is installed. Launch the Haxchi DS game each time you boot to enable CFW, then drop homebrew into `wiiu/apps` on the SD card.

Want CFW to load automatically on every boot without launching the game? Follow the **CBHC** guide to extend this Haxchi install (read its brick warnings carefully first).
